methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $55M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Radical AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $55M to date. The founding team includes Joseph F. Krause (CEO, former Army Research Lab neuromorphic semiconductor researcher and deep-tech investor), Jorge Colindres (President, ex-AlleyCorp), and Prof. Gerbrand Ceder (Chief Science Officer, UC Berkeley materials science professor). The robotics and manufacturing sector encompasses 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ); notable private benchmarks include Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method. Using the $55M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $138M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable: while 7 robotics and manufacturing peers exist, none satisfied narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables), yielding insufficient datapoints for reliable median estimation.

The single-method analysis supports a valuation range of $117M (low) to $158M (high), with a median of $138M. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable Seed-stage robotics companies at this capital-raise level and dependence on stage-progression multipliers, which may not fully capture the technical differentiation or market timing specific to neuromorphic robotics applications.
